q1_sink_dangerous.evidence: The sink is HttpResponse(...) which only constructs an in-memory response object; no network I/O, shell execution, or external side effects.

q2_observable_after_return.evidence: The XSS violation (missing escaping of dashboard_id) is embedded in the returned HttpResponse object's content and can be inspected after the function returns.

q3_result_in_memory.evidence: returns.kind is 'value' with expr 'HttpResponse('Dashboard ' + str(dashboard_id))', allowing in-memory inspection of the response content attribute.
